what_it_does: This file is a terrain-entry ledger record for the Company orienteering map. It describes the graph-editor repository (also called RelaTesseract / rltsrct MCP), summarizing what the project is, its architectural ideas, its live MCP role, its connections to other Company territories, and the evidence and limits of the current read.
observations: The repo hosts two identities: a browser-based Project Vi memory-graph editor at localhost:8899 and an MCP server (rltsrct) exposing the same EditorAPI via jsdom on ports 8898/8899.
The editor is mid-migration from a 16,000-line index.html monolith to a registry-driven, token-cascading, component-composed architecture with 18 registries across 6 layers.
The live MCP fronts 234 tools behind a 6-tool discovery facade (rltsrct, execute_tool, query_tools, get_tool_schema, list_surfaces, help).
Key design principle cited: relations are first-class and 'things only exist in meaning from the difference between things.'
Address-based coordination uses schemes such as graph://, supabase://, src://, data://, docs://, ci://, mcp-server://.
The file notes the repo is both a prospecting resource (relation:resource) and an active operational dependency (also_relation: dependency) referenced by Claude Code config.
README contains an unresolved merge conflict between two names/framings: 'RelaTesseract - graph as execution substrate' versus 'ReleTesseract — a universal and relational data store viewer.'
File count is inflated by .git/objects and .discovery/runs/ JSON dumps; hand-authored surface is much smaller.
Secrets scan found none committed, only .env.example files.
The repo also contains other MCP servers (vi-sync-mcp-server, tools/agent-dev-tracers/) that were not characterized here.
standouts: Graph treated as execution/coordination substrate, with entity and relation types encoding workflow flow.
Live rltsrct MCP is an active operational dependency, not just a historical artifact.
Address-based shared medium rather than message-based agent coordination.
Relational primitive framed as the root design axiom.
JIT 234-tool discovery facade as a concrete pattern for large MCP surfaces.
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry frontmatter schema: type, name, register, aliases, status, coverage, relation, kind, path, dates, git metadata, themes, concepts, tags.
Uses markdown section headings for What it is, Key ideas, What it connects to, When/where, Notes/evidence.
Records both a descriptive relation and an operational dependency annotation in the same entry.
Cites exact file paths and line ranges for claims.
Flags its own read limits and misleading metrics explicitly.
concerns: Status is 'unconfirmed' with only 6 of 19,890 files read.
README has an unresolved merge conflict, so the project's own name/purpose is internally inconsistent.
The live MCP dependency claim is based on config/PID/log references rather than direct reading of the 266 KB server.js body.
Other MCP servers in the same repo were not characterized, leaving overlap unknown.
File-count metric is misleading and could distort importance assessment.
notes: A dual-purpose orienteering entry that treats a Project Vi graph editor both as a source of architectural ideas and as a live MCP dependency for the Company.
questions: How exactly do the browser editor and the MCP server share state: same process, same files, or a bridge?
What do the 234 tools do, and how are they grouped in the catalog?
How is the live MCP registered in Claude Code and what triggers it?
What is the intended resolution of the README naming conflict between RelaTesseract and ReleTesseract?
Do vi-sync-mcp-server and agent-dev-tracers duplicate or complement rltsrct?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is framed as a neutral descriptive terrain entry, but its actual content also acts as an operational-dependency note, blending resource cataloging with live-system dependency tracking.
intention_signals: The entry seems to be trying to surface a sibling Project Vi tool as both an idea mine and an active piece of the Company's agent infrastructure, arguing that relational graphs can serve as the shared medium for coordination between humans, UI, and agents.
novelty: A single repository combines a visual graph editor and its own MCP server, exposing the same EditorAPI to both browser and agent; it further hides a very large tool surface behind a tiny discovery facade and treats graph relations as executable flow rather than mere metadata.
contribution: It preserves the dual identity of the repo (editor plus live MCP), the relational and address-based design principles, the JIT tool-discovery pattern, and the explicit operational-dependency link, giving a fused system orienteering context for integration decisions.
summary_for_embedding: A terrain-entry ledger record describing the graph-editor / RelaTesseract / rltsrct MCP repository, its registry-driven graph editor, live MCP server, relational design principles, and its role as both idea source and operational dependency for the Company.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to orient the Company map around a Project Vi graph tool that doubles as a live MCP dependency and a source of relational/address-based architectural patterns.
